sn15.14:1.1: Near Sāvatthī. There the Blessed One said:
sn15.14:1.2: “From an inconceivable beginning comes the wandering-on. A beginning point is not discernible, though beings hindered by ignorance and fettered by craving are transmigrating & wandering on.
sn15.14:1.3: A being who has not been your mother at one time in the past is not easy to find.…
sn15.14:1.5: From an inconceivable beginning comes the wandering-on. A beginning point is not discernible, though beings hindered by ignorance and fettered by craving are transmigrating & wandering on. Long have you thus experienced stress, experienced pain, experienced loss, swelling the cemeteries—enough to become disenchanted with all fabricated things, enough to become dispassionate,
